name: Up Somborne
overview: Up Somborne is a small village/hamlet in the civil parish of King's Somborne in the Test Valley district of Hampshire, set on rising chalk downland about 1.5 miles east of King's Somborne village and roughly 3 miles south-east of Stockbridge. It lies between Stockbridge, Romsey and Winchester (the city is about 6-7 miles east). It is a scattered, predominantly residential settlement of houses and farms along Strawberry Lane and surrounding lanes, with no shops or pub of its own; the parish church, primary school and pub are in King's Somborne. The wider parish lies in the Test Valley close to the River Test.
character: A quiet, rural downland settlement of detached houses, cottages and farmsteads strung along narrow lanes with open countryside views. The atmosphere is peaceful and agricultural, with footpaths across the downs and a strong community focus on King's Somborne village nearby.
amenities: No shop or pub in Up Somborne itself
Primary school, church and pub in King's Somborne (~1.5 mi)
Market town facilities in Stockbridge (~3 mi)
Full shopping and services in Winchester (~6-7 mi) and Romsey

commutes.notes: Via the A3057/A30 or downland lanes; mainline station to London Waterloo
From Winchester station (SWR), drive ~18 min to the station

prices.summary: Up Somborne and the wider King's Somborne parish are a sought-after Test Valley location; housing is mostly detached and period properties, with prices typically in the upper-£400k to £1m+ range and larger country houses well above. Supply is limited.

thingsToDo: Walking the Test Valley and Clarendon Way footpaths
Fishing and riverside walks on the River Test near Stockbridge
John of Gaunt's deer park and downland walks around King's Somborne
placesToEat: The Crown Inn, King's Somborne (~1.5 mi)
Pubs and cafes in Stockbridge (~3 mi), including The Greyhound on the Test
Wide choice of restaurants in Winchester (~6-7 mi)
pros: Quiet downland setting in a desirable Test Valley parish
Village primary, pub and church a short distance away in King's Somborne
Good road access to Winchester, Stockbridge and Romsey
Excellent walking, fishing and countryside on the doorstep
cons: No shops, pub or school in Up Somborne itself; car essential
Limited and relatively expensive housing stock
No railway station within walking distance
whoItSuits: Buyers seeking a quiet rural Test Valley village with good schooling and countryside nearby, within easy driving distance of Winchester's mainline and amenities.

sources.label: Ofsted - King's Somborne CofE Primary School
